title: Sprint 4 deepen High Pass into a full chapter
summary: Sprint 4 should make High Pass feel like a real next chapter by hardening chapter-state composition, deepening relationship teaching, adding one more remembered spatial payoff, and sharpening the current Route v2 replay/support feel without widening the shell.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-1 High Pass chapter-state hardening pass.
execution_order.why_now: The live next chapter should not broaden further until its filed-season and expedition state resolves through a smaller seam.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-2 High Pass relationship-teaching pack.
execution_order.why_now: High Pass now exists as a chapter shell, but it still needs one relationship-first ecology pass to feel distinct and educational.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-3 High Pass chapter-side spatial payoff.
execution_order.why_now: Stone Shelter gives the chapter a remembered middle, but the climb still wants one more memorable frame beyond that basin.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-4 High Pass route refinement pass.
execution_order.why_now: The current High Pass outing works, so the next spend should deepen its replay and support feel rather than open a second route.

guardrails: Deepen the live High Pass chapter before opening season three, biome six, or another major shell.
Keep station, expedition, journal, and Route v2 growth compact and handheld-safe at `256x160`.
Prefer one more relationship-teaching pass, one more remembered place, and one sharper replay seam over denser notebook prose or broader UI.
Land at least one more protective extraction from `field-season-board.ts`, `game.ts`, or an adjacent chapter-state seam before further chapter expansion.

main_311_focus.chapter_state_target: Harden the live High Pass / filed-season chapter state so outing locator, continuity copy, atlas note, launch card, and expedition card all derive from one compact helper instead of regrowing inline resolver logic.
main_311_focus.protective_split_target: Extract the repeated High Pass next-chapter composition out of `src/engine/field-season-board.ts` into a dedicated helper such as `src/engine/high-pass-chapter-state.ts`, leaving only thin adapters behind.
main_311_focus.watch_item: Only add a dedicated filed-season chapter status if the expedition shell truly needs another explicit state; prefer a smaller composition helper first, and leave the existing `treeline-high-pass` dormancy guard in `field-requests.ts` alone unless one tiny shared boolean is genuinely needed.
verification_targets: High Pass stays dormant while the calm season-close return is still pending.
The chapter reads cleanly once it becomes live from the current station, atlas, and expedition surfaces.
The refactor reduces inline chapter-state composition instead of introducing another dashboard seam.
non_goals: Do not add another field-station page or planner row.
Do not widen the expedition shell into a dashboard.
Do not reopen unrelated route-board or nursery copy.
Do not invent a broader chapter framework if a High Pass-specific helper is enough.

main_312_focus.chapter_target: One relationship-first High Pass content pass that teaches shelter, exposure, wind, rime, and foothold ecology through the current chapter carriers instead of a wider fact dump.
preferred_surfaces: one note-backed relationship explanation for the `rime-mark` plus `talus-hold` seam
existing ecosystem-note prompt fallback before any new authored seed

relationship_note_target.summary_direction: On exposed High Pass ground, wind and rime favor the lowest mats and lichens while tiny rock pockets still give them a place to hold.
relationship_note_target.prompt_direction: What here still holds on where rime reaches first?

non_goals: Do not make the existing `hoary-marmot` or `dwarf-birch` cards denser just because the chapter is new.
Do not add another comparison allowlist entry unless the note fallback proves insufficient.
Do not widen the atlas, journal shell, or comparison allowlists.
Do not reopen a broader alpine or tundra content wave in this chunk.

main_313_focus.chapter_target: One additional High Pass spatial payoff beyond Stone Shelter by turning the current rime-mark crest band into a real exposure-release overlook instead of ending the chapter as one good basin plus a route card.
main_313_focus.preferred_zone: late dwarf-shrub through the lee shelf into the first exposed lichen-fell
preferred_space: one compact Rime Brow overlook anchored around the current rime-cap and crest-brow band
optionally one tiny recoverable return step only if the reveal needs rhythm after the overlook reads
non_goals: Do not spend more authored density inside the current Stone Shelter basin band (`x 320-372`).
Do not add another lift immediately to the right of the open-fell island.
Do not touch the tundra corridor door or travel logic.
Do not add a new traversal family, climbable, or text surface.

main_314_focus.chapter_target: Deepen the live `treeline-high-pass` outing so the replay/support seam feels more distinct in play without opening a second High Pass route.
main_314_focus.support_target: Spend one more compact support or carrier consequence inside the current `stone-lift -> lee-watch -> rime-mark -> talus-hold` chain while keeping route-marker, note-tabs filing, and the station shell unchanged.

preferred_proof_band.zone: late dwarf-shrub into first lichen-fell lee-pocket rime shelf

replay_target.priority: Make the middle `rime-mark` band feel more distinct through the existing alternate-carrier or support seam rather than only a title change.

main_314_focus.structural_watch: If more deferred next-season requests appear later, move the dormancy guard into request metadata or a shared selection seam instead of stacking more request-id checks.
non_goals: Do not add a second High Pass route.
Do not add another station page, planner row, or route-board shell.
Do not reopen a broad weather/day-part framework pass.
Do not change the route away from the current clue-backed filing path.
